observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget
Officer prepare and submit to COA complete financial reports, which
includes the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts, and
the related registries within the prescribed period for audit/verification. The
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer should maintain the
four (4) registries as required in Section 4(h) of the Manual on NGAS to
monitor appropriations, allotments received and obligations incurred against
said allotment and in order to prevent incurrence of obligations in excess of
allotments received or approved appropriations. Said registries shall also be
the basis in preparing the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual
Amounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal
Treasurer submit the above-enumerated vouchers in accordance with the
provisions of Section 7.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and Section 347 of
R.A. No. 7160 for evaluation/audit purposes.

3.6 We reiterated our recommendations in the prior years that:
3.6.1 The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer submit the
Financial Statements, Official Receipts and Disbursement Vouchers for
CY 2018 and all the reports/documents as enumerated;
3.6.2 Management determines the cause of delay and address the same in
order to comply with the required submission dates of accounts and
reports.
3.6.3 Management assigns additional personnel in the Treasury and the
Accounting Office to facilitate prompt submission of accounts and
reports.
3.6.4 The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer exert all
necessary efforts to render the agency’s accounts within the prescribed
period.

We recommended that Management retrace all transactions prior to 2010
which might have affected the cash in bank balance in the Financial
Statements as well as cashbooks to determine the cause of the noted
discrepancies.

We recommended that Management submit the RIS, SSMI as well as the
inspection report which includes the construction materials to support the
propriety of the journal entries made. Henceforth, all requisition and
issuance of inventory be supported with properly accomplished RIS and
SSMI for the preparation of JEV to support charges made to the inventory
accounts and the recording to the appropriate expenditure accounts.

We recommended that Management/Inventory Committee conduct actual
physical count of inventories every semester, prepare and submit the Report
of Physical Count of Inventories, and reconcile results with the property and
accounting records. Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards be maintained
for effective handling/control of inventories for fair presentation of the
account in the financial statements.

7.6 We recommended the following:
7.6.1 That the Disbursing Officer/Accountable Officers regularly submit the
Report of Disbursements / Liquidation documents to the Accounting
Office for timely recording in the books of accounts for fair
presentation of the financial statements. Likewise, refund any
unexpended balance of cash advance at the end of the year as
prescribed under existing regulations;
7.6.2 That the Municipal Accountant requires the Disbursing
Officer/Accountable Officers to promptly submit the liquidation
documents and record the liquidations on time to ensure that all
transactions are included in the financial statements. Liquidations
shall be recorded only when properly supported with liquidation
report and supporting documents; and
7.6.3 No cash advance should be granted to an accountable officer unless
the previous cash advance given to him has been fully liquidated or
properly accounted for.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant takes extra care in
recording transactions and effect the necessary adjusting entries to present
fairly the respective account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management submit written explanations for the
above noted deficiencies on the grant of cash advance as well as in the
disbursement of funds, and submit the entitlement for government funds
enumerated in Item 4.4 of COA Circular No. 2007-001, and the monitoring
report on the success of project implementation.

We recommended that Management submit immediately the lacking
documents for audit/evaluation. Henceforth, the Municipal
Accountant see to it that succeeding transactions be properly
supported with complete documentation and in accordance with
pertinent laws, rules and regulations in order to establish the validity
and propriety of the transactions and disbursements. Processing and
payment of claims that are not properly documented shall be
discontinued.

We recommended that Management submits written justification for the
above noted deficiencies and submit Official Receipts or Sales Invoices as well
as the duly notarized SPA, Board/Partnership Resolution, or Secretarys
Certificate, whichever is applicable, for the above-stated payee, for audit
purposes. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant require payees to issue corresponding official receipts or sales
invoices (for goods) and sign the receive portion of the DVs for best interest of
the municipality and pursuant to COA Circular No. 2004-006, Section 237 of
the NIRC and RMC No. 2-2014.

We recommended that Management justify the foregoing observation.
Likewise, exert effort in hiring qualified personnel who will occupy the
vacant position of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer immediately deposit the
amount cash counted on December 10, 2018. Henceforth, the Municipal
Treasurer adheres to the above-mentioned laws and rules that governs the
deposit of collections. Lastly, the Municipal Mayor see to it that the
Municipal Treasurer fulfill her duties and observe the preceding audit
recommendations.

15.6 We recommended the Management requires the Municipal Treasurer to
immediately submit the original copies of the cancelled checks. Henceforth,
the Municipal Treasurer should see to it that checks are properly recorded in
the check register as a basis for the preparation of the RCI. The spoiled
checks shall then be attached to the RCI and forwarded to the accounting
department for monitoring and recording.

15.7 Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant  Designate monitor
and require from the Municipal Treasurer the submission of RCI and its
supporting documents.

We have recommended that management submit justifications for the noted lapses as well as require the designated Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert all efforts to submit the 2016 lacking reports, disbursement vouchers, and liquidation papers. 

Henceforth, Management was advised to submit all the financial reports on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for verification purposes. Further, we reiterated our recommendation that Management consider the hiring of a qualified Municipal Accountant in as much as this is a mandatory position under Sections 470 and 474, Book III, Title V of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.reason: Reiterated in part II of this report

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and that Management strengthen its internal control with regards to the grant, utilization and submission of liquidation documents.

Management was advised to issue demand letters to those personnel who failed to submit the corresponding liquidation of their cash advances. Moreover, certification of no outstanding cash advance from the Municipal Accountant should be sought before any grant of cash advances could be made.

Moreover, management should submit the required List of Delinquent Taxpayers, as well as the aging of RPT/SET Receivables together with justification as to whether a letter demanding payment thereof was issued by the concerned personnel.  

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate should look into the nature and composition of the dormant receivables account of the LGU. In the event that the composition of the said accounts could no longer be determined, filing of a request for write off of dormant receivables is in order. 

Henceforth, pursuant to Section 6.1 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should regularly monitor and analyze the receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected as they become due and demandable and that cash advances and fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose.

Also, the Municipal Accountant should henceforth prepare on a quarterly basis a schedule of all receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfer to be submitted to this office for review and validation purposes.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses and that:
The MPDC sees to it that henceforth all relevant cost for the implementation of the SWMP is integrated in the Annual Budget as well as Annual Investment Plan. Coordination among the different offices is hereby advised;
The Municipal Mayor convenes the SWMB and seeks justification as to the reason for the low success rate for the implementation of SWM projects and failure to follow the SWMP; and
The members of the SWMB revisit the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003. Henceforth, implement quality assurance/monitoring mechanism to facilitate an efficient and effective implementation of the programs indicated in the SWMP.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management submit justifications for the noted lapses as well as require the designated Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert all efforts to submit the 2017  disbursement vouchers, and liquidation papers.

Henceforth, Management was advised to submit all the financial reports on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for verification purposes. Further, we reiterated our recommendation that Management consider the hiring of a qualified Municipal Accountant in as much as this is a mandatory position under Sections 470 and 474, Book III, Title V of the Local Government Code of 1991.

In view of the aforegoing observations, we have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and that Management strengthen its internal control with regards to the grant, utilization and submission of liquidation documents.

Management was also advised to issue demand letters to those personnel who failed to submit the corresponding liquidation of their cash advances. Moreover, certification of no outstanding cash advance from the Municipal Accountant should be sought before any grant of cash advances could be made.

Moreover, management should submit the required List of Delinquent Taxpayers, as well as the aging of RPT/SET Receivables together with justification as to whether a letter demanding payment thereof was issued by the concerned personnel.

Also, we have further recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate should look into the nature and composition of the dormant receivables account of the LGU. In the event that the composition of the said accounts could no longer be determined, filing of a request for write off of dormant receivables is in order.

Henceforth, pursuant to Section 6.1 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should regularly monitor and analyze the receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected as they become due and demandable and that cash advances and fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose.

Lastly, the Municipal Accountant should henceforth prepare on a semestral basis a schedule of all receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfer to be submitted to this office for review and validation purposes.

In order to establish an accurate and reliable balances of all municipal properties recorded in the books and forestall losses as well as to determine actual conditions and existence of properties we have recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor create a committee to conduct a yearly physical inventory of PPE that will ensure that a Report on Physical Count of PPE to the Accounting Office be prepared and submit on or before January 31st of the ensuing fiscal year
The Municipal Treasurer/ property custodian ensure regular renewal/updating of Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment (ARE);
The Property custodian on or before January 31st of the ensuing fiscal year submit Report on Physical Count of PPE to COA for evaluation;and
The Municipal Agriculturist to render report on the status and whereabouts of the recorded animals/livestock.

We have recommended that Management provide justification as to the above noted observation. Likewise, expedite the verification/authentication of the Municipal of the records and exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU.

Also, ensure that the Municipal Accountant together with the Property Custodian make proper reconciliation between the balance per book of accounts as against the balance of land per authenticated record of the Municipal Assessor.

We have recommended that the management retrace all transactions prior to 2010 which might have affected the cash in bank balance in the Financial Statements as well as cashbooks to determine the cause of the noted discrepancies.

In view of the above noted observations, we have recommended to Management the following:
The respective personnel in charge of the noted deficiencies to submit the required supporting documents; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to verify whether all necessary supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before processing the claims, otherwise the claim should be returned to the respective employee/personnel for compliance of the requirements.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the lacking supporting documents. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant sees to it that all disbursements are properly supported with the required documentations. Likewise, the materials and quality tests to ensure the quality of the works of the contractors should be made mandatory.

In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended that management instruct the Accountant to submit the necessary supporting documents and ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly documented and supported with complete documentation.  The noted lacking documents shall be submitted to COA for evaluation and conduct of necessary inspection.

We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses. Management should identify the person/s liable for the unremitted obligations and take necessary actions to recover the amount of interest incurred. Henceforth, management sees to it that future related charges to be made are in consonance with the approved Annual Budget of the LGU.
We had recommended that Management justify the foregoing observation. Likewise, exert effort in hiring a qualified treasurer in order for the Municipal Accountant to assume her previous position.

In view of the above noted observation, we have recommended that management justify the reimbursements made by the LGU officials Henceforth, refrain from using reimbursement as method of payment for procurement unless there are unforeseen events which will justify the immediate payment thereof.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant justify the payment of the transactions despite the absence of the BAC Resolution recommending the mode of procurement used and award of the contract.

Moreover, we have recommended that the management identifies the method of procurement used and justifies the non-conduct of competitive public bidding.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Bids and Awards Committee revisit the provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and henceforth, abide to the said rules and regulations.

We have recommended that the BAC see to it that invitations are sent to various observers three (3) days before the conduct of the eligibility checking, shortlisting, pre bid conference, preliminary examination of bids, bid evaluation and post qualification. Said invitations should be properly filed and attached together with the contracts submitted so that the team will be able to determine management’s compliance with the said promulgation

We have recommended that management submit the required supporting documents needed to verify whether the BAC had actually conducted pre-procurement conference in any. Henceforth, the BAC should ensure that the conduct of pre-procurement conference is properly documented and that such documentations are attached to the contract documents submitted to this office.

We have recommended the management to submit the copy of the clippings for the advertisement in a newspaper of nationwide circulation if any; otherwise justify the reason for the failure to adhere to provisions of above cited regulation.

We have recommended that management henceforth adhere to the required number of days between the conduct of pre-bid conference as well as bid opening. Likewise, the required number of days between the PhilGEPs posting and the pre-bid conference should also be followed

We have recommended that the BAC justify the noted observation. Henceforth, before accepting the bidding documents for the suppliers/ contractors see to it that the amount of bidding documents are properly paid.

We have recommended that the BAC to justify the above noted observation. Submit additional documents if any. Henceforth, see to it that all the detailed engineering designs be completed before the conduct of public bidding.

We have recommended that the BAC justify the above noted observation submit the lacking documents the soonest if any since said documents should form part of the contract documents to be validated by the audit team. Henceforth, the BAC should prepare a checklist of documentary requirements needed for the bidding process so as not to miss any important documents during the eligibility checking

We have recommended that the BAC justify the noted lapses and provide additional information/ documentation so as to ascertain the validity of the post-qualification of the process conducted. Henceforth, the BAC should be more vigilant in the conduct and scrutiny of the documents being submitted by the suppliers/ contractors

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the required performance security if any. Henceforth management should see to it that the contractor with the lowest calculated and responsive bid had submitted a performance security before entering into contract.

We have recommended that the management justify the above noted observation, henceforth see to it when CARI is being required under the GCC and SCC, the BAC should also require the contractor to submit said documents.
We have recommended that the BAC use the 5th edition of the Philippine Bidding Documents. They should see to it that they are kept updated with every promulgations being issued by the GPPB.

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, see to it that after verifying the documents which would form part of the contract, the BAC properly affix their signature so as to ensure that no alterations will be made upon the submitted documents.

In view of the above noted lapses, we have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses and that:
The MPDC sees to it that henceforth all relevant cost for the implementation of the SWMP is integrated in the Annual Budget as well as Annual Investment Plan. Coordination among the different offices is hereby advised;
The Municipal Mayor convenes the SWMB and seeks justification as to the reason for the low success rate for the implementation of SWM projects and failure to follow the SWMP; and
The members of the SWMB revisit the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003. Henceforth, implement quality assurance/monitoring mechanism to facilitate an efficient and effective implementation of the programs indicated in the SWMP.

We have recommended that management rectify the noted lapses. Prepare and submit the urban/sub-urban greening plan as well as the accomplishment report of the LGU detailing the number of seeds planted and its survival rate. Likewise, in compliance with E.O. 26, the establishment of nurseries is highly recommended.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following concerned personnel:
The Municipal Treasurer thru the help of the Local Sanggunian to open a separate depository account solely for the receipt and utilization of the Share from Tobacco Excise Tax. Upon creation of which, the Municipal Accountant should maintain special account under the General Fund.
Henceforth, the concerned department head of the unimplemented projects evaluate the viability of the implementation thereof. Develop a time line thru which the projects shall be implemented.
Management submit documents pertaining to the lot wherein the structure was built.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the Sangguniang bayan Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC as well as the LCPC-CLJIP, if any. Also, management clarify the charges made to the fund without any identified reference document and submit reconciled accomplishment report together with the required supporting annexes. Henceforth, ensure that management observe and abide with the rules and regulations as regards the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

In view of the above noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
For the utilization of the lump-sum appropriation, the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer submit justification as to the reason for allowing the disbursements despite absence of re-alignment or additional appropriation ordinance;
For the unimplemented projects, the Municipal Engineer consult the duly prepared Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for infrastructure projects, if any, since said report include the timeline for the implementation  of the project. Justification as to the reason for the non-implementation also be submitted; and
Lastly, we have recommended that management revisit DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1, as amended so as to familiarized themselves with the main purpose of the said fund

In view of the foregoing observations, we have recommended that the Management:
Justify the non-creation of LDRRM Office, non-hiring of the required staff, non-institutionalization of various policies and plans, non-conduct of vulnerability and adaptation assessment as well as the discrepancy in the status of the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF in the previous years;
Provide the liquidation documents for the received in-kind goods and locally procured goods
Submit LDRRM Plan as well as realignment of funds or SB authorization for the items procured not in conformity with the LDRRMFIP
Improve the internal control system of the LGU in order to ensure that the PPAs charged to the fund are for LDRRM related activities
See to it that unexpended/ unobligated funds from prior years LDRRM are treated as Trust Liability at the end of the year and duly transferred to a special trust fund account
Utilize the prior years’ trust liability for the acquisition of disaster preparedness equipment as well as immediate assistance and services to calamity stricken constituents
Henceforth, provide in the Notes to Financial Statements all the required disclosures as mandated under COA Circular No. 2014-002
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2018 onwards.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Engineer designate is also advised to continuously update himself with the new guidelines for infrastructure projects and henceforth abide with the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 11 S. 2014.

In view of the noted lapses, we have recommended the following:
The Municipal Mayor creates a GFPS Execom, TWG and Secretariat that will supervise, advocate and install a GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) as well as the required Gender  Responsive Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) System
The Municipal Mayor with the help of the Sangguniang Bayan, formulate and adapt a Local GAD Code
The MSWDO being the leading authority in identifying gender related issues and concerns, see to it that GAD activities are identified through the use of HGDG and GAD Database, that the GAD plan are regularly updated and that the identified projects do address the GAD issues and concern in the long run.
Henceforth, management should follow the prescribed format of GAD plan and see to it that said plan and budget are approved by the DILG and the PPDO.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instructs the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to locate and submit the missing disbursement vouchers. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant should see to it that disbursement vouchers together with the supporting documents be submitted to the auditor’s office

We have recommended that the management:

Submit the duly accomplished RIS for all medicines and SMSI as well as the liquidation documents for the purchased agricultural products
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant record as inventory all medicines purchased regardless of funding source. Also, before recording the drugs and medicines expenses, require the corresponding RIS from the RHU as support for the SMSI

We have recommended that the Municipal Assessor verify and/or authenticate the records provided by the claimants with regards to the questioned lands. Likewise, Management exhausts all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU.

We have recommended that the management retrace all transactions prior to 2010 which might have affected the cash in bank balance in the Financial Statements as well as cashbooks to determine the cause of the noted discrepancies

We have recommended the following:

The respective personnel in charge submit the required documents
Henceforth, The Municipal Accountant and Treasurer verify whether all necessary supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before the processing of claims
All disbursements not attached with the required supporting documents should not be process and should be returned to the respective employee/ personnel

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant justify payment of the claims in spite of the non-submission of the documents. Likewise, require the Municipal Engineer to submit the needed documents for verification and evaluation of the reported accomplishments of the contractor. Henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by R.A. No. 9184.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer require the submission of an irrevocable standby letter of credit or surety bond before the release of the 15% mobilization fee.

We have recommended that Management require the contractors to post additional warranty security for those which are still within the 1 year defect liability period. Henceforth, oblige all contractors to post the required warranty securities
We have recommended that Management justify the foregoing observation. Likewise, exert effort in hiring a qualified treasurer in order for the Municipal Accountant to assume her previous position.
We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses and henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

We have recommended that management justify the purchase of second hand heavy equipment despite its non-inclusion in the LDRRM Investment Plan or submit the amended LDRRM Investment Plan if any. Likewise, justify the purchase of second hand heavy equipment through negotiated procurement without conducting technical study.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the management to justify the noted lapses.  The Bids and Awards Committee should revisit the provisions of R.A. 9184 and henceforth, abide by the said rules and regulations.

We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, DPWH Department Order No.22 s.2015 should be utilized in the computation of the ABC of infrastructure projects. Likewise, the members of the Municipal Engineering familiarize themselves with the promulgations issued by the DPWH

We have recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee justify the noted lapses, submit the needed documents for verification and henceforth, comply with the rules and regulations as required by R.A. No. 9184

We have recommended that management enforce the collection of the liquidated damages amounting to P 196,537.40. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer should inspect the projects with substantial delay to determine the actual accomplishment of the contractor and terminate the contract as well as recommend the forfeiture of the performance security, if warranted.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Sangguniang Bayan pass a resolution authorizing the Municipal Treasurer to open a separate depository account for the Shares of the LGU from Tobacco Excise Tax. Upon creation of which, the Municipal Accountant should maintain special account under the General Fund.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the required documents and henceforth familiarized themselves and abide by the rules and regulations set forth by the source agencies.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the urban/ sub-urban greening plan if any as well as the accomplishment report of the LGU detailing the number of seeds planted and its survival rate. Likewise, in compliance with E.O. 26, the establishment of nurseries is highly recommended.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the Sangguniang bayan Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC. Submit the LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan for CY 2015 if any. Henceforth, management  should observe and abide to the rules and regulations with regards to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.

We have recommended the management justify the noted lapses. Submit duly approved re-alignment of funds by the Sangguniang bayan and Sangguniang Panlalawigan. Moreover, it is recommended that the creation of special trust fund account be made for the unobligated/ unexpended balance of the LDRRMF. Henceforth management is advised to familiarize themselves with relevant rules and regulations pertaining to the utilization of the 5% LDRRMF.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Engineer designate is also advised to continuously update himself with the new guidelines for infrastructure projects and henceforth abide to the provisions of DPWH Dept. Order No. 11 S. 2014

We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses. Henceforth, GAD Plan be submitted to the Provincial Planning and Development Office for review. Likewise, GAD plan and report should be accomplished using the prescribed format.

We have recommended the following:

The concerned officials and employees justify the noted lapses otherwise these will be disallowed in our audit
Submit the itinerary of travels and certificate of appearances for the paid travelling expenses with incomplete supporting documentation
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer review the supporting documents of the claims for travelling expenses.

We have recommended that management submit justifications for the noted lapses as well as require the designated Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert all efforts to submit the 2016 lacking reports, disbursement vouchers, and liquidation papers.

Henceforth, Management was advised to submit all the financial reports on or before the 10th day of the ensuing month for verification purposes. Further, we reiterated our recommendation that Management consider the hiring of a qualified Municipal Accountant in as much as this is a mandatory position under Sections 470 and 474, Book III, Title V of the Local Government Code of 1991.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and that Management strengthen its internal control with regards to the grant, utilization and submission of liquidation documents.
Management was advised to issue demand letters to those personnel who failed to submit the corresponding liquidation of their cash advances. Moreover, certification of no outstanding cash advance from the Municipal Accountant should be sought before any grant of cash advances could be made.
Moreover, management should submit the required List of Delinquent Taxpayers, as well as the aging of RPT/SET Receivables together with justification as to whether a letter demanding payment thereof was issued by the concerned personnel.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant-Designate should look into the nature and composition of the dormant receivables account of the LGU. In the event that the composition of the said accounts could no longer be determined, filing of a request for write off of dormant receivables is in order.
Henceforth, pursuant to Section 6.1 of COA Circular No. 2016-005, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer should regularly monitor and analyze the receivable accounts to ensure that these are collected as they become due and demandable and that cash advances and fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending upon their nature and purpose.
Also, the Municipal Accountant should henceforth prepare on a quarterly basis a schedule of all receivables, unliquidated cash advances, and fund transfer to be submitted to this office for review and validation purposes.

We have recommended that Management justify the above noted lapses and submit proof that said items were really delivered by the suppliers. In the event that said items were actually delivered by the suppliers, the whereabouts as well as the status of the said items should be reported and presented for audit.
Any items which could no longer be used due to improper storage thereof should be refunded by the officials following the basic rule that accountable officers  found negligent shall be liable for loss of government funds or property inas provided under Section 105(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445, to wit:
Every officer accountable for government property shall be liable for its money value in case of improper or unauthorized use or misapplication thereof, by himself or any person for whose acts he may be responsible. He shall likewise be liable for all losses, damages, or deterioration occasioned by negligence in the keeping or use of the property whether or not it be at the time in his actual custody. (Emphasis supplied)

We have recommended that Management provide justification as to the above noted observation. Likewise, the Municipal Assessor was advised to verify and/or authenticate the records provided by the claimants with regards to the questioned lands. The Management should exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the LGU.

Furthermore, the Municipal Accountant together with the Property Custodian was advised to see to it that a proper reconciliation is made between the balance per book of accounts as against the balance of land per authenticated record of the Municipal Assessor.

We have recommended that the management retrace all transactions prior to 2010 which might have affected the cash in bank balance in the Financial Statements as well as cashbooks to determine the cause of the noted discrepancies.

We have recommended to Management the following:
The respective personnel in charge of the noted deficiencies to submit the required supporting documents; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to verify whether all necessary supporting documents are attached to the disbursement vouchers before processing the claims, otherwise the claim should be returned to the respective employee/personnel for compliance of the requirements.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and submit the lacking supporting documents. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer require the submission of an irrevocable standby letter of credit or surety bond before the release of the 15% Mobilization Fee and Retention Money. Likewise, the materials and quality tests to ensure the quality of the works of the contractors should be made mandatory.

We have recommended that management instruct the Accountant to submit the necessary supporting documents and ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly documented and supported with complete documentation.

The noted lacking documents are advised to be submitted to COA for evaluation and conduct of necessary inspection. Henceforth, the Municipal Engineer should see to it that all disbursements should not exceed the ABC of the project.

We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses. Management should identify the person/s liable for the unremitted obligations and take necessary actions to recover the amount of interest incurred. Henceforth, management sees to it that future related charges to be made are in consonance with the approved Annual Budget of the LGU.

We have recommended that management justify the non-payment of the terminal leave benefits of the six (6) employees. Henceforth, the management was advised to prioritize the payment of terminal leave benefits of the employees for them to enjoy their rightful benefits.
We had recommended that Management justify the foregoing observation. Likewise, exert effort in hiring a qualified treasurer in order for the Municipal Accountant to assume her previous position.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses and henceforth, adhere to the transparency provisions as provided by the different rules and regulations cited above.

We have recommended that Management justify the noted lapses and that:
The MPDC sees to it that henceforth all relevant cost for the implementation of the SWMP is integrated in the Annual Budget as well as Annual Investment Plan. Coordination among the different offices is hereby advised;
The Municipal Mayor convenes the SWMB and seeks justification as to the reason for the low success rate for the implementation of SWM projects and failure to follow the SWMP; and
The members of the SWMB revisit the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003. Henceforth, implement quality assurance/monitoring mechanism to facilitate an efficient and effective implementation of the programs indicated in the SWMP.

We have recommended that management rectify the noted lapses. Prepare and submit the urban/sub-urban greening plan as well as the accomplishment report of the LGU detailing the number of seeds planted and its survival rate. Likewise, in compliance with E.O. 26, the establishment of nurseries is highly recommended.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the required documents and henceforth familiarized themselves and abide by the rules and regulations set forth by the source agencies.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the Sangguniang bayan Resolution or Administrative Order creating the LCPC. Submit the LCPC Annual Work and Financial Plan for CY 2016, if any. Henceforth, management should observe and abide with the rules and regulations with regard to the formulation of budget and utilization of the 1% LCPC fund.
We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Mayor instruct the MPDC to discuss with the LDC the preparation and adoption of both plans for CY 2017 onwards.

We have recommended that the management justify the noted lapses. With regard to the unfinished infrastructure project, management should determine the appropriate action to be taken as against the contractor for its failure to complete the infrastructure projects on time while funding the continuation and finalization of the Mapisla Bridge should be prioritized by management. Henceforth, strengthen the internal control with regard to project monitoring and supervision to ensure proper implementation of projects.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. The Municipal Engineer designate is also advised to continuously update himself with the new guidelines for infrastructure projects and henceforth abide with the provisions of DPWH Department Order No. 11 S. 2014.
We have recommended that the agency submit a copy of Annual Procurement Plan (APP) of the agency for 2015 and henceforth, an APP should be prepared and approved before any procurement is made.
observations.managementAction: Management submitted a copy of its APP for CY 2015

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management  instruct the accountant and treasurer to submit the necessary supporting documents and ensure that all disbursement vouchers are properly documented and supported with complete documentation.  The noted lacking documents shall be submitted to COA for evaluation and conduct of necessary inspection. Henceforth, the Municipal Engineer should see to it that all disbursements should not exceed the ABC of the project
observations.managementAction: Management submitted the APP for CY 2015

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

For the procured construction materials, the Municipal Engineer submit proof that said items were really delivered by the suppliers. In the event that said items were really delivered by the suppliers, the whereabouts as well as the status of the said items should be reported and presented for audit

Any items which could no longer be used due to improper storage thereof should be refunded by the officials following the basic rule that accountable officers  found negligent shall be liable for loss of government funds or property in as provided under Section 105(2) of Presidential Decree No. 1445

For the utilization of the lumpsum appropriation as well as the utilization of funds without re-alignment, the Municipal Budget Officer, Accountant and Treasurer submit justification as to the reason for allowing the disbursements despite absence of re-alignment or additional appropriation ordinance

For the unimplemented projects, the Municipal Engineer should consult the duly prepared Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for infrastructure projects if any, since said report include the timeline for the implementation  of the project

For the failure to fully utilize the newly created 20% Development Account, the Municipal Accountant and treasurer are advised to exercise due diligence in preparing checks for payment of projects  to avoid erroneous charges to the general fund proper

Lastly, we have recommended that management  revisit DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 on the expenditure items covered and prohibited to be paid out of the 20% Development Fund for guidance.

We have recommended that management justify the noted lapses. Submit the required documentary requirements and familiarize themselves with various rules and regulations with regards to the fund transfers to various NGOs/POs.
observations.managementAction: Fund transferred to one of the Kalahi Community account had already been liquidated

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management explain the reason of incurring additional government contributions in excess of the prescribed limits otherwise the amount P 147,851.96 shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Monthly deductions in the payroll of the LGU personnel with overly paid HDMF contributions were being made.
